# Child Friendly Salford Web

This repository contains the front-end codebase for the **Child Friendly Salford** website.  
The site supports the local delivery of **UNICEF UK’s Child Friendly Cities and Communities programme**, providing clear, accessible information for families, young people, organisations and partners across Salford.

---

## 📌 Project purpose

Child Friendly Salford aims to:
- Promote and embed **children’s rights** locally
- Support children and young people to be **heard, safe, active and empowered**
- Encourage collaboration between families, organisations, schools, community groups and the private sector
- Provide a central, trusted source of information about the programme

This website is designed as a **public-facing, institutional platform**, aligned with local council and UNICEF UK standards.

---

## 🚧 Project status

**Current status:**  
✔️ **Minimum Viable Product (MVP)**

The site is fully functional as a static website and suitable for:
- Academic submission
- Internal review
- Stakeholder demonstrations
- Early public-facing use

Some features are intentionally designed as **placeholders for future expansion**.

---

## 🧩 Site structure

/
├── index.html # Homepage
├── about.html # About the programme
├── families.html # Information for families & young people
├── organisations.html # Information for organisations & partners
├── news.html # News & updates
├── contact.html # Get involved / contact
├── assets/
│ ├── css/
│ │ └── style.css # Global styles
│ ├── images/ # Images used across the site
│ └── js/
│ └── main.js # Minimal JavaScript (navigation, utilities)
└── README.md # Project documentation

---

## 📝 Content approach

- **Tone:** clear, inclusive, institutional (UK English)
- **Audience:**  
  - Children and young people  
  - Families  
  - Organisations, schools, community groups  
  - Private sector partners
- **Design principles:**  
  - Accessibility-first  
  - Consistent visual hierarchy  
  - Neutral, non-commercial imagery  
  - No promotional branding from private companies

---

## 📄 Forms section (important)

On the homepage, there is a section labelled **“Forms”**.

### What it does now
- Acts as a **placeholder** for future participation and feedback tools
- Currently redirects users to **Get involved / Contact**
- Prevents broken links or non-functional forms

### Why this is intentional
At the MVP stage:
- There is **no backend**
- No data collection workflows have been approved
- No CMS is in place

This approach:
- Keeps the site honest and usable
- Signals **future scalability** without over-promising functionality

---

## 🔮 Future enhancements (planned / possible)

The project has been structured to support future development, including:

### 1. Forms & participation tools
- Feedback forms for children and families
- Organisation and partner registration forms
- Surveys and consultations
- Secure data handling (GDPR compliant)

### 2. CMS integration
- Migration to WordPress or similar CMS
- Editable content for News and Updates
- Image and document management

### 3. News & updates expansion
- Individual news pages
- Event listings
- Image galleries linked to real activity
- Archive and filtering

### 4. Accessibility enhancements
- Formal WCAG 2.1 AA audit
- Enhanced keyboard navigation
- Optional text-size controls
- Easy-read content versions

### 5. Multilingual support
- Translated key pages
- Language toggle
- Support for families where English is not a first language

---

## 🤝 Contribution notes

This repository is intended to be:
- Easy to understand for new contributors
- Safe to extend without breaking existing content
- Clear about what is **implemented** vs **planned**

Before adding new features:
- Review existing structure and styles
- Maintain consistency with UNICEF UK and council standards
- Avoid adding commercial branding or sponsorship content

---

## 📬 Contact

For questions about the programme or the website, users are directed to the **Contact / Get involved** page until more advanced features are implemented.

---

## 📄 License / usage

This project is for **educational and demonstration purposes** and reflects a real-world institutional web approach.  
Content and branding should not be reused without appropriate permission.

---

*End of README*
